sourceMovie.title: Volcano
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Volcano works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Godzilla
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Volcano clicked for you, Godzilla (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Volcano clicked for you, San Andreas (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Volcano?
faq.answer: Try Godzilla, San Andreas, 10 Cloverfield Lane—each shares DNA with Volcano while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Volcano?
faq.answer: Volcano (1997) is best described as Science Fiction, Action, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Volcano worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
